prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on the Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, which assumes stock prices follow a log-normal distribution. Historical daily adjusted close prices for DE over the last 5 years were used to estimate the daily drift (mean log return) and daily volatility (standard deviation of log returns). For the 21-day forecast period, these parameters were then used to calculate the median of the projected log-normal distribution as the point estimate. Confidence intervals were derived by calculating the corresponding bounds for the log-prices (using standard normal Z-scores for 50%, 80%, and 90% confidence levels) and then exponentiating these bounds to translate them back into price ranges. The current price of $629.71 was used as the starting point for the projection.
